$comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for capability negotiation. Each capability groups tools that share a Chrome permission / scope; it is derived CONCEPTUALLY from tools.json (permission + scope). On connect, the extension/host advertise which capability `id`s are actually available (permission granted, tool enabled) and the server negotiates against this list. A tool is callable only if its capability is advertised. `permissions` are the Chrome manifest permissions the capability needs; `tools` are the tool names (from tools.json) it covers. Keep in sync with tools.json when tools are added.

capabilities.description: Enumerate, focus, open, close browser tabs and capture the visible viewport. Opening/closing are gated by allowlist + user confirmation.
Capture the active tab's interactive elements as an accessibility-style tree via the content script (fast approximation, ~90% coverage).
Capture the authoritative accessibility tree via Chrome's debugger (CDP Accessibility.getFullAXTree). Requires the debugger permission and briefly shows the 'Started debugging this browser' banner.
Drive the page: click elements, fill form fields, scroll, and wait for conditions. High-risk clicks trigger an on-page confirmation Toast.
Read the active tab's visible text content (sensitive fields masked).
Execute arbitrary JavaScript on the active tab. Highest-risk capability: every call requires explicit user approval and return values are masked by default.
Read cookies (including httpOnly) for allowlisted hosts. Read-only; values are masked. Backed by chrome.cookies in the service worker.
Read the active tab's localStorage / sessionStorage (same-origin only). Read-only; values are always masked.
